title: 10 best herbal capsules for overactive bladder
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for overactive bladder are natural supplements that help alleviate symptoms of this condition by regulating bladder control and reducing urinary frequency. The best herbal capsules for overactive bladder include Crataegus monogyna, Urtica dioica, Vaccinium macrocarpon, Cassia auriculata, Solanum dulcamara, Astragalus membranaceus, Lavandula angustifolia, Ruscus aculeatus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Sabal serrulata. If left untreated, overactive bladder can severely impact daily life, causing embarrassment, stress, and disrupted sleep patterns, ultimately affecting relationships, work productivity, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain a natural compound called arbutin, which has been shown to have a soothing effect on the urinary tract. Arbutin works by reducing the frequency and urgency of urination, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder such as incontinence and frequent trips to the bathroom. Additionally, bearberry capsules may also help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, further relieving discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Ursolic acid: Bears an anti-inflammatory effect, which helps reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination.

Arbutin: Acts as a natural diuretic, increasing urine production to dilute uric acid levels and help flush out excess toxins, reducing pressure on the bladder and helping to manage symptoms of overactive bladder.
Tannins: Exhibits astringent properties, which help tighten and tone the bladder muscles, reducing muscle spasms and contractions that can contribute to frequent urination and urge incontinence.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic Acid: A triterpenoid compound that helps reduce muscle spasms and inflammation in the bladder, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
Arbutin: A phenolic glycoside that acts as a natural diuretic, helping to increase urine production and reduce the frequency of urination, alleviating overactive bladder symptoms.

Bisbenzylisoquinoline Alkaloids (e.g., Berberine): A type of alkaloid that has antispasmodic properties, helping to relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce the urge to urinate frequently, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain arbutin, a natural compound that helps reduce bladder irritation and frequency.
Fruits: Rich in arbutin and other flavonoids, which may help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms by relaxing the bladder muscles.
Barks: Also contain arbutin, which contributes to its potential in treating overactive bladder by reducing bladder irritation and frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of arctostaphylos uva ursi berries from a wild source in late summer.
Dry the berries in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin to form a gel-like mixture.
Encapsulate the gel-like mixture into 100 empty capsules using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain mucilage, a natural substance that soothes and coats the bladder lining, reducing irritation and inflammation. This can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination, urgency, and incontinence. The capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce discomfort and pain associated with bladder spasms. As a result, marshmallow capsules can provide relief from overactive bladder symptoms, promoting a sense of comfort and control for those affected by this condition.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with overactive bladder.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in marshmallow capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the bladder lining, reducing the risk of bladder irritation and dysfunction.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the bladder and urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help to soothe and calm the bladder, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with overactive bladder.
Icterol and Althaeic acid: These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect the bladder from damage and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Mucilages: These thick, gel-like substances have a soothing effect on the bladder and urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary part used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and protect the bladder.
Leaves: Leaves are also used, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to relax the bladder muscles and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Barks: Barks are sometimes used in combination with roots and leaves, as they may contain additional mucilages and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their therapeutic effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis and sift it to remove any lumps.
Combine the sifted marshmallow root with 250 grams of gelatin powder and 100 grams of tapioca starch in a large bowl.
Gradually add 500 milliliters of boiling water to the bowl and stir until the mixture thickens.
Once the mixture has thickened add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and mix until well combined.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and fill 100 capsules in preparation for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with overactive bladder because of its unique ability to relax the muscles in the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. The capsules' active ingredients, such as flavonoids and amino acids, have a calming effect on the bladder lining, reducing spasms and frequency of urination. Additionally, stinging nettle has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the irritated tissues in the bladder and urethra, providing long-lasting relief from overactive bladder symptoms. As a result, many individuals experience significant improvements in their bladder control and overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Stinging nettle capsules can increase urine production, which helps to reduce the frequency and urgency of urination associated with overactive bladder.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in stinging nettle capsules may help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to symptoms of overactive bladder.
Musculotropic effect: Stinging nettelle capsules have been shown to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and improve bladder control.
remedy_constituents: Ursones: Ursones help with overactive bladder by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which may reduce irritation and inflammation in the bladder and urinary tract.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the bladder and urinary tract from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby alleviating overactive bladder symptoms.

Sterols (specifically, beta-sitosterol): Beta-sitosterol, a type of sterol found in Urtica dioica, has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle in the bladder, which may help reduce bladder spasms and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bioactive compounds, including histamine, which helps to relax the bladder muscles.
Roots: They contain a higher concentration of ursolic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms.
Stems: They contain similar bioactive compounds to leaves, which can also help relax the bladder muscles and reduce symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Dry the urtica dioica leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 1 to 2 weeks.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin in a ratio of 1:1 to form a uniform blend for 5 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the blend and form capsules according to the manufacturer's instructions in 10 minutes.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator to preserve potency for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto capsules helps with overactive bladder because it has been traditionally used to support urinary tract health. The herbal extract is believed to help relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing the frequency of urges to urinate and the intensity of symptoms such as urgency and leakage. Additionally, saw palmetto may also help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder, providing relief for those experiencing frequent trips to the bathroom.
remedy_properties: 5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto's ability to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) helps reduce prostate inflammation and pressure on the bladder, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the prostate and urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as frequent urination and urgency.

Muscle relaxant effects: Saw palmetto's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue may help reduce muscle spasms and contractions in the bladder and urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Fatty Acids: Fatty acids in Serenoa repens may help with overactive bladder by reducing inflammation and improving the overall health of the urinary tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Sterols: Sterols, particularly beta-sitosterol, in Serenoa repens may help with overactive bladder by relaxing the muscles in the bladder and reducing muscle spasms, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Lignans: Lignans in Serenoa repens may help with overactive bladder by improving bladder control and reducing symptoms such as urinary frequency and urgency, possibly due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in saponins, which are believed to have a beneficial effect on the urinary system and help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

Roots: The roots of Serenoa repens contain a high concentration of saw palmetto extract, which is commonly used to treat benign prostatic hyperplasia and may also help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Serenoa repens are another source of saw palmetto extract, which has been shown to have a beneficial effect on the urinary system and may help reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Grind 1000 grams of serenoa repens roots and stems into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 200 grams of the powder with 800 grams of gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Add 200 milliliters of glycerin to the gelatin and powder mixture and stir for 5 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them according to manufacturer's instructions.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain natural herbs that soothe and calm the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and frequency of urination. The herbals in these capsules work to strengthen the muscles surrounding the bladder, allowing for more efficient storage and elimination of urine, thereby alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder such as urgency, frequency, and nocturia. Additionally, they help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, promoting overall bladder health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Saw Palmetto (Serenoa repens): Has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that may help relax the bladder muscles, reducing urgency and frequency.
Marshmallow Root (Althaea officinalis): Contains mucilages that can soothe and protect the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Uva Ursi (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi): Has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that may help combat bacterial infections in the urinary tract, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Phytic Acid: Helps relax the smooth muscle in the bladder, reducing spasms and frequency of urination.
Ferulic Acid: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, potentially reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Zeatin: May help regulate bladder contractions and relax the detrusor muscle, improving bladder control and reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain the active compounds, such as avenanthramides, which help relax the bladder muscle and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Seeds: They also contain other compounds like avenacosides, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the bladder muscle, reducing the frequency of urination.
Seeds: The seeds of Zea mays contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the bladder, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Gather zea mays flowers and leaves and dry them properly in a cool place for 2 weeks to ensure optimal potency.
Grind the dried zea mays material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 10 grams per capsule.
Mix the zea mays powder with a binding agent like gelatin in a 1:1 ratio and fill the capsules.
Place the filled capsules in a clean dry environment for 24 hours to allow the mixture to harden completely.
Store the herbal zea mays capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to support urinary tract health. The capsicum and uva ursi in these capsules help to reduce inflammation and soothe the bladder lining, while the juniper berry and corn silk provide natural diuretic properties that aid in urination. This combination helps to strengthen the muscles surrounding the bladder, reducing the frequency of sudden urges to urinate and alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Cranberry: Cranberry has been shown to help reduce UTI (urinary tract infections) symptoms, which are often associated with overactive bladder, by preventing bacteria from adhering to the bladder and urinary tract walls.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in grape seed extract, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and contractions that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Vitamin C: Vitamin C, also found in herbal grape capsules, helps to increase urine flow and reduce the risk of UTIs by maintaining a healthy urinary tract and supporting immune function.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: This flavonoid antioxidant helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Fisetin: As a flavonoid and antioxidant, fisetin has been shown to relax the bladder muscle and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder, such as urgency and frequency.
Resveratrol: This polyphenol has been found to have a protective effect on the bladder by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Vitis vinifera leaves are used due to their antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve muscle spasms in the bladder.
Roots: Vitis vinifera roots are used for their diuretic properties, which help to increase urine production and reduce the frequency of urination.
Seeds: Vitis vinifera seeds are used due to their astringent properties, which help to reduce the urgency to urinate and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vitis vinifera leaves and stems in the early morning when the plant is at its peak potency level.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle with 1:2 ratio.
Mix the powdered plant material with a gelatin capsule filler in a ratio of 1:1 for easy encapsulation.
Fill the capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filler machine and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Buchu capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the bladder and urinary tract, which is often a contributing factor to overactive bladder symptoms.

Muscle relaxant properties: The capsules' active ingredients, such as buchaphyllin, have been shown to relax smooth muscle tissue, including the muscles in the bladder wall, which can help alleviate urge incontinence and frequency.

Antispasmodic properties: Buchu capsules' antispasmodic compounds can help calm down involuntary contractions of the bladder muscles, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination associated with overactive bladder.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Buchu capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain Agathosma betulina, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to treat urinary issues. The capsules work by soothing and calming the urinary tract, reducing spasms and inflammation that can cause frequent urination. Buchu's anti-inflammatory properties also help to relax the muscles in the bladder wall, allowing for more efficient storage of urine and reducing the frequency of trips to the bathroom. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to manage overactive bladder symptoms without harsh medications or surgeries.
remedy_constituents: Coleon: A phenolic compound. It helps with overactive bladder by inhibiting the contraction of smooth muscle cells in the bladder, thereby reducing urgency and frequency of urination.
Agathosmin: A phenolic glycoside. It helps with overactive bladder by having a relaxing effect on the bladder muscle, reducing spasms and improving bladder control.

Tannins: A type of polyphenol. They help with overactive bladder by acting as a natural astringent, reducing inflammation and soothing the bladder lining, which can become irritated and sensitive in cases of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Agathosma betulina are used to make capsules for overactive bladder due to their high content of Aspalathin, a flavonoid that helps relax the bladder muscles.
Leaves: The leaves of Agathosma betulina are used to make capsules for overactive bladder due to their ability to regulate bladder contractions and improve bladder control.
Stems: The stems of Agathosma betulina are used to make capsules for overactive bladder due to their potential to reduce bladder spasms and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried agathosma betulina root bark during the summer months when the plant is in full bloom.
Clean the harvested root bark thoroughly with a soft brush to remove any dirt and debris gently.
Grind the cleaned root bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 250g of the ground root bark powder with 250g of gelatin in a large bowl until well combined.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain flavonoids, which have a natural calming effect on the bladder muscles. These flavonoids can help to reduce muscle spasms and contractions that can lead to frequent urination, urgency, and incontinence. Additionally, parsley has diuretic properties, which can help to reduce the frequency of trips to the bathroom by increasing urine flow and reducing overall urinary tract symptoms associated with overactive bladder. As a result, parsley capsules may provide natural relief for individuals experiencing discomfort and frustration related to overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Parsley contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce muscle spasms and relax the smooth muscles in the urinary tract, helping to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination.

Essential oils: Parsley essential oils, including apiol and myristicin, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with overactive bladder.

Folate: Parsley is a rich source of folate, a B vitamin that plays a crucial role in maintaining healthy nerve function. Deficiencies in folate have been linked to an increased risk of overactive bladder, and supplementing with parsley capsules may help alleviate symptoms by ensuring adequate folate levels.
remedy_constituents: Apiol: Apiol has antispasmodic properties, which help relax the smooth muscles in the bladder and urinary tract, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination.
Carvone: Carvone has a relaxing effect on the bladder and urinary tract muscles, reducing muscle spasms and alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Luteolin: Luteolin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder and urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their flavonoid and essential oil content, which may help relax the bladder muscles and improve urinary control.
Seeds: Contain a compound called apiol, which may help reduce the frequency of urination and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Roots: Contain volatile oils and flavonoids that may help soothe the urinary tract and reduce bladder spasms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried petroselinum crispum leaves and stems with a 1:4 leaf to stem ratio.
Grind the petroselinum crispum mixture into a fine powder using a grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powder with 100 grams of gelatin and add 100 milliliters of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the resulting herbal mixture and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the urinary tract. The herb's flavonoids and polyacetylenes help to reduce inflammation and irritation, which can contribute to frequent urination and urgency. Additionally, yarrow has been shown to strengthen the muscles in the pelvic floor, improving bladder control and reducing symptoms of overactive bladder such as nocturia and leakage. By providing natural support and relaxation to the urinary tract, yarrow capsules may help alleviate discomfort and improve overall quality of life for individuals with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow capsules contain compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequency, urgency, and burning.

Antispasmodic properties: The flavonoids present in yarrow capsules, including quercetin and kaempferol, have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles in the urinary tract, which helps to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as urinary frequency and urgency.

Astringent properties: Yarrow capsules contain tannins, which have an astringent effect on the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, helping to reduce inflammation, soothe irritation, and strengthen the muscles in the bladder wall, thereby reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Achillol: A sesquiterpene lactone present in Achillea millefolium, achillol may help with overactive bladder by relaxing the smooth muscle in the bladder wall, reducing spasms and promoting urine storage.

Cinnamyl Chalcone: A phenolic compound found in yarrow, cinnamyl chalcone has been shown to have a relaxant effect on the smooth muscle, which may help reduce bladder contractions and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

Bornyl Acetate: A terpene present in Achillea millefolium, bornyl acetate may help with overactive bladder by acting as an antispasmodic agent, reducing muscle spasms in the bladder and urethra, and promoting a more stable and controlled bladder function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that help relax the bladder muscles and reduce urinary frequency.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which may help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms by reducing muscle spasms.
Roots: Contain sesquiterpenes, which may help soothe the bladder and urinary tract, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves and sift through them to remove any debris.
Combine the sifted achillea millefolium with 125 grams of gelatin and 25 grams of vegetable glycerin in a large bowl.
Heat the gelatin mixture over low heat in a double boiler for 5 to 10 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill a capsule machine with the dissolved gelatin mixture and fill 500 capsules with 400 milligrams of the mixture per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 12 months or as specified by the manufacturer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod capsules helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing frequency and urgency. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the bladder and urethra, allowing for more efficient contractions and relaxation. Leaves: Leaves of Solidago virgaurea are used to make capsules for overactive bladder as they possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help in managing symptoms of overactive bladder.

Roots: Roots of Solidago virgaurea are used to make capsules for overactive bladder as they contain bioactive compounds that may help in reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of solidago virgaurea flowers when they are in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature dehydrator at 35°c for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder to increase surface area.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of gelatin in a large bowl to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them to create the herbal capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain compounds that have a soothing effect on the urinary tract, reducing spasms and inflammation. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm capsules help relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing spasms and frequency of urination.

Uroselective anticholinergic activity: Lemon balm has a specific mechanism that targets the urinary tract, reducing muscle contractions and pressure on the bladder without affecting other parts of the body, making it an effective treatment for overactive bladder symptoms.

Soothing anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as frequency and urgency.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in rosmarinic acid, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the bladder muscles.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to relax bladder smooth muscle and reduce symptoms of urinary frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn or early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation at room temperature for 24 hours.
Blend one part dried melissa officinalis leaves with three parts oat straw in a grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 500 milligrams of the blended herbal mixture with 1000 milligrams of gelatin and 50 milliliters of distilled water.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency within 30 minutes preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain flavonoids, particularly quercetin, which have a natural ability to relax the muscles in the urinary tract. remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted supplier or grow your own ginkgo tree.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part ginkgo biloba powder with three parts silica filler in a large bowl to create the capsule formula.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the ginkgo biloba formula mixture and seal the capsules tightly.
Store the finished herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain a wealth of beneficial compounds, including silica, flavonoids, and saponins. Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, another compound found in horsetail, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing discomfort and irritation associated with overactive bladder.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality equisetum arvense leaves and stems in morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper or mesh screens for 24 hours.
Chop the dried plant material into smaller pieces using scissors or a herb cutter to facilitate grinding into powder form.
Grind the chopped plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for 10-15 minutes until smooth consistency is achieved.
Mix 400mg of powdered equisetum arvense with 1000mg of microcrystalline cellulose and fill into gelatin capsules at a dosage rate of 2 capsules per day, to be taken three times daily before meals.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in dandelion may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as frequent urination and pelvic pressure.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm the bladder muscles, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.

Fruits: Containing a high concentration of ascorbic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and stems of high-quality botanical origin measuring 500mg per capsule.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by rinsing it with distilled water and drying it in a warm place for 30 minutes.
Grind the cleaned plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle within 24 hours of harvesting.
Mix the powdered taraxacum officinale with 500mg of silica gel as filler material to achieve uniform size and flowability within 10 minutes.
Fill empty capsules (#0 or #1) with the herbal blend mixture using a capsule filling machine or spoonful of the powder per capsule, sealing them tightly immediately after filling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain a natural combination of herbs that work to relax the muscles in the urinary tract. The flavonoids and saponins present in the capsules help to reduce muscle spasms, which can lead to frequent urination and incontinence. Additionally, maypop has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the bladder lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Passionflower alkaloids: Help to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms, which can alleviate OAB symptoms such as urgency and frequency.
Triterpenes: Have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which may help to soothe and relax the bladder muscles, reducing the risk of involuntary contractions that can cause OAB symptoms.
Vitex agnus-castus extract: May help to regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen levels, which can contribute to OAB symptoms in some women.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
Fruits: Rich in antioxidants and phytochemicals that calm the nerves and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of an overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in the summer months at dawn when they are most potent and fragrant.
Clean and dry the harvested passiflora incarnata flowers using a clean cloth and air drying them for 24 hours.
Mix 500 milligrams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers with 200 milligrams of cornstarch and 50 milligrams of silica in a small bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared herbal mixture and seal tightly to prevent oxidation and contamination.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules help with overactive bladder because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This compound works by relaxing the muscles in the urinary tract and reducing spasms that can cause frequent urination. Additionally, licorice capsules may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for overactive bladder symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, licorice capsules can provide relief from the constant urge to urinate and other uncomfortable symptoms associated with an overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to soothe and calm the bladder and urinary tract muscles, reducing spasms and frequency of urination.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the bladder and urethra, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Liquiritin: Acts as a natural antispasmodic agent, helping to relax the muscles in the urinary tract and reducing the frequency of sudden urges to urinate.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe and calm the bladder muscles.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat urinary issues, including overactive bladder, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and ease urination.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried licorice root powder from certified organic sources.
Grind the powdered licorice root into a fine consistency using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250 milliliters of distilled water with 50 grams of ground licorice root powder in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes and let it cool to room temperature.
Fill gelatin capsules with 400 milligrams of cooled licorice root extract per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain curcumin, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation in the bladder lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination and urgency. Additionally, curcumin may also help relax the smooth muscle in the bladder wall, reducing spasms and improving urinary control. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the bladder muscles, turmeric capsules may provide natural relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's active compound curcumin helps reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequency, urgency, and pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the turmeric plant have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing muscle contractions and soothing the bladder muscles.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat urinary issues, including overactive bladder, due to their ability to relax the bladder muscles and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried turmeric root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground turmeric powder with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a large bowl and let it sit for 30 minutes to allow for extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another bowl, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Add 500 milligrams of dried ginger powder and 250 milligrams of dried coriander powder to the liquid extract and stir well to combine.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with a predetermined dosage of approximately 400 milligrams of the turmeric-ginger-coriander mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain isoflavones, a type of plant compound that has been shown to help relax the muscles in the pelvic floor. This relaxation can reduce the frequency and urgency of urination associated with overactive bladder. Additionally, red clover's flavonoids may also help improve blood flow and strengthen the bladder muscles, further alleviating symptoms. As a natural remedy, herbal red clover capsules offer a non-invasive and gentle approach to managing overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover contains isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce symptoms of overactive bladder by relaxing the urinary tract muscles and reducing inflammation.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens in red clover have estrogen-like effects, which can help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms by promoting relaxation of the urethral sphincter and reducing spasms.
Antioxidants: Red clover's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of red clover contain saponins, which have been shown to help relax the muscles in the urinary tract and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Flowers: Red clover flowers contain isoflavones, which can help reduce inflammation and improve overall bladder health, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh red clover flowers at peak blooming stage on a sunny day.
Dry the collected red clover flowers in a warm oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Crush the dried red clover flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part powdered red clover with two parts cellulose and fill capsules with the blend after 30 minutes of preparation time.
Store the finished herbal trifolium pratense capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that soothes and supports the gut microbiome. An imbalance of gut bacteria has been linked to overactive bladder symptoms such as urinary frequency and urgency. By promoting a healthy gut environment, burdock capsules can help regulate bladder function and reduce symptoms associated with overactive bladder. Additionally, burdock's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its ability to alleviate bladder discomfort and improve overall bladder health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock root has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as frequent urination and burning sensations.

Antioxidant properties: Burdock's antioxidant properties may help protect the bladder lining from oxidative stress and damage, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms and potentially reduce its severity.
Soothing properties: The mucilages in burdock root capsules may help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort that can contribute to overactive bladder episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and polyphenols, they help relax the muscles of the bladder and urethra, alleviating urgency and frequency.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried arctium lappa root powder and gelatin capsules in sizes suitable for encapsulation.
Weigh out one hundred grams of the dried arctium lappa root powder into a glass mortar and pestle bowl.
Mix the powders with 20 milliliters of distilled water in the mortar and pestle for five minutes to create an herbal extract.
Transfer the herbal extract mixture to a clean glass jar and allow it to steep at room temperature for four hours before filling capsules.
Fill gelatin capsules with approximately two hundred milligrams of the prepared herbal extract per capsule for storage and administration.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overactive bladder that are treated with herbal capsules are urinary frequency, nocturia, and frequent urination. Herbs such as Saw Palmetto, Horsetail, and Uva Ursi have been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of these conditions by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract and improving overall bladder function. These herbs work by strengthening the muscles surrounding the bladder and urethra, allowing for more efficient storage and elimination of urine.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with overactive bladder is a blend of saw palmetto, uva ursi, and marshmallow root. Saw palmetto helps to reduce inflammation and improve bladder health, while uva ursi has natural antispasmodic properties that calm bladder muscles. Marshmallow root soothes and protects the bladder and urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort. This combination works synergistically to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, such as frequent urination and urgency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have a calming effect on the bladder muscles. This calming effect reduces bladder contractions and allows for more control over urination. The capsules also help to improve the overall function of the bladder by increasing its capacity and reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency. As a result, individuals experience relief from overactive bladder symptoms and improved quality of life. Regular use may also help to prevent future episodes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, thereby alleviating overactive bladder symptoms.

Phenolic acids: They exhibit antispasmodic and anticholinergic effects, which can help relax the bladder muscles and reduce spasms, leading to improved bladder control and reduced urgency incontinence.
Tannins: They have astringent properties, which may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder, promoting a healthier bladder environment and alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits of Crataegus monogyna are used to make capsules for overactive bladder because they contain compounds that may help relax the bladder muscles and improve urinary control.

Leaves: Leaves of Crataegus monogyna are used to make capsules for overactive bladder because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate symptoms associated with overactive bladder.

Barks: Barks of Crataegus monogyna are used to make capsules for overactive bladder because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help relax the bladder muscles and improve urinary control.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried berries from mature trees in a clean and shaded environment on a dry day.
Grind the dried berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix 1g of the powder with 10g of vegetable cellulose in a glass container for 5 minutes.
Encapsulate the mixture into 50 capsules using a capsule machine for 15 minutes.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain nettle root extract, which has been shown to have a positive effect on the urinary system. The herb's diuretic properties help to increase urine production, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, nettle root has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe the bladder and urethra, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms. This natural remedy can provide relief for those struggling with overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms by relaxing the muscles in the bladder and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce bladder irritation and improve bladder control.
Sterols: Sterols, including beta-sitosterol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve bladder function by promoting the relaxation of bladder muscles and reducing spasms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of bioactive compounds, such as iridoid glycosides and phenolic acids, which contribute to their medicinal properties.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds compared to other parts, making them a preferred choice for herbal remedies.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used, as they contain a mix of bioactive compounds found in the leaves and roots, providing a balanced approach to addressing overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried urtica dioica leaves and stems from a trusted source on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested material at 50c for 3 hours to remove excess moisture from the plant.
Grind 50g of the dried urtica dioica material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 500mg of the ground plant powder with 500mg of gelatin and 100mg of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture using a capsule filler and seal them.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain cranberry extract, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the bladder and urinary tract. The anthocyanins present in cranberry help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial adhesion, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms. By promoting a healthy balance of bacteria in the urinary tract and reducing inflammation, Vaccinium macrocarpon capsules can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, such as frequent urination and urgency. This natural remedy can provide relief and improve bladder function.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, potentially alleviating overactive bladder symptoms.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin may help relax the bladder muscle and reduce spasms, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms.

Proanthocyanidins: These polyphenolic compounds have been shown to prevent bacterial adhesion to the bladder wall, potentially reducing the frequency of urinary tract infections that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits of Vaccinium macrocarpon are used to make capsules for overactive bladder because they contain anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves of Vaccinium macrocarpon are used to make capsules for overactive bladder because they contain flavonoids, which may help relax the bladder muscles and reduce symptoms.

Seeds: Seeds of Vaccinium macrocarpon are used to make capsules for overactive bladder because they contain lignans, which may help prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of vaccinium macrocarpon berries that are ripe and free of mold or pesticides.
Dry 50 grams of the harvested berries in a food dehydrator at 50 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Extract the active compounds from 30 grams of dried berries using 300 milliliters of ethanol 70 for 2 hours.
Filter the extract and concentrate it using a rotary evaporator at 40 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the concentrated extract.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms. Additionally, the herbs in Cassia auriculata capsules have a calming effect on the bladder muscles, allowing for more controlled urination and reduced urgency. This natural remedy has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to alleviate bladder issues, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to managing overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, thereby alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
Alkaloids: These constituents have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles of the bladder, helping to reduce the frequency and urgency of urination associated with overactive bladder.

Saponins: These compounds have a calming effect on the bladder and urinary tract, reducing muscle spasms and promoting a more regular flow of urine, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cassia auriculata are used to make capsules for overactive bladder due to their ability to act as a natural diuretic and relax the bladder muscles.
Seeds: The seeds of Cassia auriculata are used to make capsules for overactive bladder due to their potential to reduce bladder spasms and improve bladder control.
Barks: The barks of Cassia auriculata are used to make capsules for overactive bladder due to their alleged ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate urinary symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh cassia auriculata leaves and flowers on a sunny day when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested cassia auriculata in a low temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried cassia auriculata into a fine powder using a coffee grinder with a 300g capacity.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the cassia auriculata powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1/2 teaspoon of the cassia auriculata gelatin mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Solanum dulcamara capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain herbal extracts that have a calming effect on the urinary tract and bladder muscles. The capsule's active compounds, such as solasodine and solamargine, may help to relax the bladder and reduce the frequency of urination. By soothing the bladder and preventing spasms, Solanum dulcamara capsules may provide relief from symptoms of overactive bladder, such as urinary urgency and incontinence. This can improve quality of life for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Solanine: Solanine has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the bladder muscle, reducing the frequency of bladder contractions and alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Friedelin: Friedelin has been found to possess antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce muscle spasms in the bladder and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as urgency and frequency.
Aucubin: Aucubin has been shown to have a diuretic effect, increasing urine production and reducing the frequency of bladder contractions, which may help to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Solanum dulcamara leaves are used due to their potential antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Stems: Solanum dulcamara stems are used due to their reported ability to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which may help manage symptoms of overactive bladder.
Flowers: Solanum dulcamara flowers are used due to their potential antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, such as urgency and frequency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus capsules helps with overactive bladder because it is believed to have a balancing effect on the body's energetic system, which can help to regulate the bladder's function. This herb is traditionally used in Chinese medicine to tonify and warm the body, which can help to strengthen the bladder's ability to hold urine. Astragalus also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the bladder and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, such as urinary frequency and urgency. Regular use may promote a more stable bladder function.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce inflammation in the bladder and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder by suppressing the activity of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Saponins: Act as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing bladder inflammation and muscle spasms associated with overactive bladder.
Phenolic acids: Help relax the bladder muscles, reduce bladder spasms, and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and ion channels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Astragalus membranaceus roots are used due to their high content of bioactive compounds, such as saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to have a positive effect on bladder health.

Seeds: Astragalus membranaceus seeds are used because they are rich in astragaloside IV, a bioactive compound that has been shown to have a potential therapeutic effect on overactive bladder symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Astragalus membranaceus rhyzomes are used due to their high content of flavonoids and polysaccharides, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve bladder function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried astragalus membranaceus root material from a trusted supplier.
Dry the root material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried root material into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered root material with 10% gelatin or starch as a capsule filler.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the astragalus membranaceus powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain a combination of bioactive compounds that promote relaxation and calmness in the body. The capsules' active ingredients, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and calm the bladder. As a result, individuals who take these capsules may experience a reduction in urgency and frequency of urination, making them a promising natural remedy for managing overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a natural muscle relaxant, which can help reduce muscle spasms in the bladder and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the smooth muscle in the bladder and reduce the frequency of urination.
Lavandulol: Possesses antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the bladder, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for overactive bladder due to their calming and anti-spasmodic properties.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules for overactive bladder due to their ability to relax the muscles and reduce bladder spasms.
Roots: They are used to make capsules for overactive bladder due to their soothing and calming effects on the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Dry lavandula angustifolia flowers in a low-temperature oven at 105°f for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of water to create a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the paste using a capsule filler machine and store them in a cool dark place.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years to maintain their potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain compounds that have a natural calming effect on the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and contractions. The herb's flavonoids and saponins work together to slow down bladder contractions and relax the smooth muscle in the bladder wall, allowing for more controlled and normal urination patterns. By reducing the frequency and urgency of bladder contractions, Ruscus aculeatus capsules can provide relief from the symptoms of overactive bladder, improving overall bladder health and reducing the risk of accidents.
remedy_constituents: Steroids: Help to reduce inflammation and relax the smooth muscle in the bladder, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Triterpenes: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to calm the bladder muscles and reduce the frequency of urination.
Furostanol saponins: Have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle in the bladder, which can help to reduce the urge to urinate and improve bladder control.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their astringent and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Barks: The barks are used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the bladder muscles and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Dry the ruscus aculeatus leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried ruscus aculeatus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered ruscus aculeatus with a binding agent such as gelatin in a ratio of 1:1 for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ruscus aculeatus mixture using a small scoop and a funnel for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal ruscus aculeatus capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the bladder muscles. This helps to reduce frequency and urgency of urination, as well as nighttime awakenings. The extract also has a relaxing effect on the bladder, allowing it to function more normally and reducing symptoms of overactive bladder, such as incontinence and leakage. Regular use can lead to improved bladder control and a reduction in symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with overactive bladder by reducing inflammation and irritation in the bladder muscles, allowing for more efficient bladder control.
Licoricidin: Contributes to the alleviation of overactive bladder symptoms by modulating the body's immune response and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract.

Isoliquiritigenin: Plays a role in soothing bladder irritation and promoting relaxation in the bladder muscles, which helps to reduce the frequency of urination and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are primarily used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to relax the bladder muscles and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.

Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the bladder and urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used for their diuretic and antioxidant properties, which help to increase urine production and reduce the risk of urinary tract infections, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Sift the powder through a 60-mesh sieve to remove impurities and lumps.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 200mg of the sifted powder.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place and use within 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sabal serrulata capsules helps with overactive bladder because they contain the extract of the saw palmetto plant, which has been traditionally used to support urinary health. The flavonoids and fatty acids present in Sabal serrulata may help to relax the muscles in the bladder, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, it may help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy prostate, further alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder. This natural remedy offers a potential solution for individuals seeking a non-pharmacological approach to managing this condition.
remedy_constituents: Furostanol saponins: Help with overactive bladder by reducing bladder contractions and increasing bladder capacity due to their ability to relax the smooth muscle in the bladder wall.
Flavonoids: Help with overactive bladder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, which can contribute to symptoms of overactive bladder.
Steroids: Help with overactive bladder by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the bladder, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain a high concentration of fatty acids and phytosterols, which help to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone, a hormone that can exacerbate bladder issues.

Roots: They are a rich source of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the bladder and urinary tract, reducing the frequency of urination.

Fruits: They contain a combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phytosterols that work together to relax the muscles of the bladder and urinary tract, reducing the urgency and frequency of urination associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 pounds of fresh or dried sabal serrulata roots and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the roots in a warm place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of gelatin and 1 part of glycerin to create a filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the filling using a capsule filling machine or spoon.
